response.justification: Szabo brings valuable geospatial expertise through GISCollective and direct navigation systems experience via Green Map, plus strong distributed systems background from Dunnhumby handling millions of requests at scale. However, his last C++ work was over a decade ago (2014-2015), and his recent career has focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and high-level languages. For a mid-senior IC role requiring modern C++ or Rust for systems-level pipeline work, this represents a significant technical skill gap despite his strong general engineering foundation and relevant domain knowledge.
strengths: Deep geospatial and navigation domain expertise through GISCollective platform, Green Map GIS application, and FOSS4G speaking; direct experience building navigation guidance solutions
Proven distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby with microservices handling millions of requests/second, low-latency asynchronous I/O, and real-time data processing
Strong software craftsmanship discipline evidenced by TDD/CI/CD practices, creation of testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), infrastructure setup, and mentoring through workshops and code dojos
concerns: C++ experience ended in 2014-2015 (10+ years ago); most recent decade focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and JavaScript rather than modern systems-level C++
No Rust experience mentioned anywhere on resume; role requires modern C++ or Rust as primary requirement for performance-critical systems programming
Career trajectory shifted toward product engineering and web stacks rather than systems programming needed for routing tile optimization, latency reduction, and infrastructure-level work

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain expertise and navigation systems background
Modern C++/Rust systems programming skills gap
